**Edgar RTB System**

1. **App Structure**  
     
   The app is built as microservice, based on golang, gin framework.  
   The workflow is explained in the diagram below.  
     
   ![][image1]  
   Also can store all incoming requests to s3 bucket according to the configuration.  
     
     
   **Installation**  
- Install golang on the machine ([https://golang.org/doc/install](https://golang.org/doc/install))  
- Run and build.

  To run the app, use \`make\` command.

  *make run*

  To build the app, use below command

  	*make build\_for\_linux*


2. **Google configuration.**  
     
   Google RTB application uses google’s authorized buyer/seller’s API.  
   Detailed api guide is here ([https://developers.google.com/authorized-buyers/apis/v1.4](https://developers.google.com/authorized-buyers/apis/v1.4))  
   Here you can check google account informations, billing information related with bidder data.

First of all, should get authorized token to access google api  
It is using google OAuth playground. ([https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground](https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground))![][image2]  
Here, choose **Ad Exchange buyer API 1.4/https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adexchange.buyer**  
And input scope below input box, the scope is “[https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adexchange.buyer](https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adexchange.buyer)”

And then when you authorized google account, you can see authorized code for tokens page.![][image3]

After clicking Exchange button, you can get right access token here.![][image4]

With this access token, you can access google adbuyer api directly with postman.

Get account information  
Link: [https://www.googleapis.com/adexchangebuyer/v1.4/accounts/299038253](https://www.googleapis.com/adexchangebuyer/v1.4/accounts/299038253)

Should input access token in authorization header, bearer token  
![][image5]

Get billinginfo  
![][image6]  
Update account Info  
![][image7]  
Here you can define bidder location information including endpoint, maximumQPS, region, bidProtocol.

3. **Manage App**  
   Currently the app is running on aws ec2 ubuntu server.  
   

Configuration  
Before run app, you should set configuration correctly.  
Config file is  project\_dir / config / config.json.

{  
  "ServerConfigurations": {  
    "Port": ":8000",  
    "InstanceName": "google-rtb-aws-us-east-1"  
  },  
  "Logger": {  
    "FileLocation": "log/app.log",  
    "Level": "info"  
  },  
  "FileUploader": {  
    "ChunkSize": 10000,  
    "LocalStorageDir": "data"  
  },  
  "AWS": {  
    "Region": "us-east-1",  
    "S3": {  
      "Bucket": "google-rtb-production-us-east-1"  
    }  
  },  
  "S3Stream": false,  
  "BidURL": \[  
    {  
      "BillingID": "",  
      "URL": "https://novadsp.net/backend"  
    }  
  \]  
}

Here the main point is bidURL array and s3 setting.  
You can define bidURL array with your forwarding endpoints.  
Currently billingID is no useful.

When S3Stream is set as true, s3 request uploading will work  
FileUploader/ChunkSize define the requests number in one file being uploaded to s3 bucket.

Run  
Currently the app is registered in ubuntu system as a service  
**Start:** 	sudo systemctl start google-rtb  
**Stop:** 	sudo systemctl stop google-rtb  
**Status:** sudo systemctl status google-rtb

4. **Deploy App**  
   Currently in the ubuntu server, golang is not installed,  
   So should build the application in local dev environment and push to github.  
   On server, should pull this application and restart the app.  
     
   Domain setting.  
   The domain google-rtb-test.com is registered on aws router 53 service  
   Elastic load balancer was created for google-rtb instance, and linked to this domain.  
     
5. **Google's BillingID setting**  
   currently billingID is used to point the google-rtb app endpoint

   {  
      "kind": "adexchangebuyer\#billingInfo",  
      "accountId": 299038253,  
      "accountName": "Tuky Data Research Ltd.",  
      "billingId": \[  
          "58953981490",  
          "100578167712",  
          "104602012074"  
      \]  
   }

   this is the example google billinginfo for the account 299038253  
   so all bid requests issued by those billingids will be provided to our endpoint  
     
   Setting billingID with forwarding endpoints separately.  
   We can set it in config.json file.  
     "BidURL": \[  
       {  
         "BillingID": "...1490",  
         "URL": "https://novadsp.net/backend"  
       },  
       {  
         "BillingID": "...7712",  
         "URL": "http://edgargo-app.rtb.adp3.net/"  
       }  
     \]  
     
     
   \---END—  
     
     
     
     
     
     
     
     
     
     
     
     
     
     
   “Real-time Bidding API v1”  
   https://realtimebidding.googleapis.com/v1/buyers/299038253/creatives/32310?view=full  
     
1. Connect to [OAuth playground](https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground)  
2. Select “Real-time Bidding API v1”  
3. Click “Authorize API’s” \+ “allow”  
4. Click: “Exchange Authorization code for Tokens”  
5. Checkbox “Auto-refresh the token before it expires.”  
6. To pull all pretargeting ID’s:  
    GET https://realtimebidding.googleapis.com/v1/bidders/299038253/pretargetingConfigs  
7. To add creativeSize config, select **PATCH**  
8. Calls list [here](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Q5EGwq1SYsxMX_t6lFJs2aaZMtblgKd-67Z2hJoDBzo/edit#gid=0)
